schedule_anomalies.finding: Today's 11 AM daily social run did not fire. No report file for 2026-04-19 in monitor/social/. Scheduler shows nextRunAt = tomorrow, implying today's slot was skipped not just delayed. Last successful daily report: 2026-04-18.
schedule_anomalies.hypothesis: Scheduler may have de-prioritized a cheaper daily agent under load, OR a jitter+cron interaction slipped past the window. Not the first time a daily Sonnet agent has missed — monitor trend.
schedule_anomalies.recommendation: Human operator: consider a manual dome-social trigger to recover today's baseline, and check scheduler logs for skip reason.

schedule_anomalies.finding: Cron is `0 */4 * * *` (every 4h) but last commit from workspace-sync was 2026-04-18T15:04Z — ~20h 30min gap. Next run scheduled ~14:03Z today (still 2h 35min away). This means 4-5 expected runs have been skipped.
schedule_anomalies.impact: workspace-owned files (status.json, review-state.json, morning-briefing.txt) have not been pushed to git in >20h. Agents that clone fresh (decider, curmudgeon) rely on git, so they see STALE versions of these live-pipeline-state files. Decider reads its OWN morning-briefing from the clone — stale briefing could mean self-reinforcing confusion on the next run.
schedule_anomalies.hypothesis: Same scheduler anomaly pattern as dome-social — daily / infrequent agents being skipped. Could also be: the 2026-04-18 PROP-009r2 apply left workspace-sync disabled, or the ownership-triad expansion affected its smart_copy short-circuit. Worth investigating both.
schedule_anomalies.recommendation: Human operator: (a) manual trigger workspace-sync once to refresh the git-side state; (b) verify scheduler task config for dome-workspace-sync is still `enabled: true`; (c) check for any recent change to workspace-sync.md that introduced a crash. If this recurs, a PROP for a dead-man's-switch (alert when workspace-sync misses 2+ consecutive runs) would catch it faster.

schedule_anomalies.finding: status.json currently says 'ATTENTION: Curmudgeon interval is 30m (fast) but mode is BAU. Human should restore to 240m.' Actual curmudgeon cron is `20 * * * *` = hourly (60m), not 30m. The note is stale by one adjustment.
schedule_anomalies.recommendation: Decider writes this field on each run. On its next update, the note should read '60m (hourly)' not '30m'. Low urgency — mechanical drift, no pipeline impact. Not a tinker self-fix because status.json is workspace-owned live state; decider owns it.

claude_md_audit.pipeline_table_accuracy: CLAUDE.md uses 'Variable (30m churn-and-burn / 8h quiet)' language for analyst/curmudgeon/decider. This is INTENTIONALLY flexible (accommodates mode swings) but does not match any single snapshot. For a point-in-time reader, the tinker.md table (now updated this run) is the authoritative current-cron reference. Recommend leaving CLAUDE.md's flexible language but cross-linking tinker.md as the live-state source. Not a fix — design tension.

claude_md_audit.hardcoded_counts: None found in this audit pass. CLAUDE.md's 'Computed Counts' rule is still honored — all numeric claims in the document either reference commands to query live OR refer to structural constants (e.g., 'Eight scheduled agents').

agent_efficiency.note: When social runs, it produces substantive reports (18-34KB). But today it didn't run at all — see schedule_anomalies. A no-op would be waste; a missed run is a different failure mode (silent gap in monitoring).
Last 5 runs all produced distinct artifacts (4 on 2026-04-18 including PROP-009r and PROP-009r2, + this run's 2 self-fixes + verification of PROP-009r2). No waste.

cost_engineering.cumulative_estimated_savings: Not quantified this run; see 2026-04-18 reports for PROP-009r2 defect-prevention estimate.

recommendations.text: Investigate dome-social missed run (no 2026-04-19 report; next scheduled 2026-04-20T09:05Z, skipping today). Consider manual trigger to recover today's baseline.

Investigate dome-workspace-sync skipped runs — last push was 2026-04-18T15:04Z; 4-5 scheduled 4h slots missed. Live-state files (status.json, morning-briefing.txt) are not reaching git, so agents cloning fresh see stale pipeline state. Manual trigger recommended to catch up.

PROP-009r2 is FIXED and running cleanly (4/4 pops strict_queue_id, 0 bypass). Move status to 'closed/applied' on the proposal file. Mark PROP-009r and PROP-009 as 'superseded' explicitly if not already.
PROP-005 pre-flight gate has been STILL_BROKEN for 15+ consecutive tinker runs with no forward momentum. Recommend close-as-wontfix or re-scope.
PROP-010 (reverse git→workspace sync) remains unwritten. Today's run was lucky — FUSE all-clean — but the architectural problem persists. Schedule for next Mode 2 run.
status.json schedule_note is stale: says 'Curmudgeon interval is 30m' but cron is hourly. Decider should refresh this field on its next run when updating status.

other_side_counter_arguments.claim: Two self-fixes in one Mode 3 run looks like scope creep
other_side_counter_arguments.counter: Both are strictly additive, documentation-only, and one was explicitly recommended by the prior report. The tinker.md update is a self-fix the prompt EXPLICITLY invites ('If the table above is wrong, update it'). Both were verified mechanical, zero-risk, and save the next tinker from re-discovering the same drift.

other_side_counter_arguments.claim: Declaring PROP-009r2 FIXED after only 4 pops is premature
other_side_counter_arguments.counter: True — 4/4 is not a statistically strong sample. The FIXED verdict applies to the apply-success question (did enforcement actually turn on), not the long-term-stability question. The flip criteria explicitly require 7 consecutive clean days (Md-3 weekly audit) before declaring victory. Report wording distinguishes 'apply verified' from 'stability confirmed.'

other_side_counter_arguments.claim: Workspace-sync and social skipped-runs could be routine scheduler jitter, not anomalies
other_side_counter_arguments.counter: 4+ missed 4-hour slots is not jitter — jitter on this system is ≤10min. And social's nextRunAt is tomorrow, not 'a few hours late today' — the scheduler has committed to skipping. Worth escalating even if the root cause turns out to be benign, because a silent skip is indistinguishable from a silent failure.

other_side_counter_arguments.claim: The disk trend (83% flat for 6+ days) is fine — no action needed
other_side_counter_arguments.counter: Flat ≥80% usage is 'one bad run from full,' per the tinker dispatcher's own threshold. The reason it has been flat is that agents are deleting clones correctly after use. If a single agent crashes after clone but before delete, we lose the cushion. PROP-010 discussion is partly about recovering disk headroom. Not urgent but not safely ignorable.

notes_to_future_tinker: Self-fixes this run were pushed to git AND copied to FUSE manually — tinker.md (0eb75894) and CLAUDE.md (a9127eb). If workspace-sync was broken (it was!), this manual sync is the only way the FUSE-reading agents see the update until workspace-sync is restored.
PROP-009r2 7-day clean window: apply date is 2026-04-18T14:35Z, so earliest declaration-of-stability is 2026-04-25T14:35Z. Schedule that Mode 3 run as the Md-3 bypass-tuple audit.
If dome-social stays missed for 2+ consecutive days, that escalates from moderate to major. Check again on 2026-04-20.
CLAUDE.md is now 148 lines. Prior Mode 3 cap discussion was ~200L. Still comfortable headroom, but any further additive bullet should come with a prune-candidate check.

The schedule_anomalies finding for workspace-sync is the more urgent of the two — the file 'monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.jsonl' itself is git-owned and pushed via build.js publish, but the workspace-sync agent is what keeps monitor/status.json / review-state.json / morning-briefing.txt fresh in git. If workspace-sync is down, the decider's morning-briefing loop can desync.

Priority-queue pops since PROP-009r2 enforce: 4. I recorded the full (qid, pop_reason, claimed_review_file) tuple in prop_009r2_verification.history_detail — future tinker can do diff-since-last to track new bypass events.
